subject: Cumulative and Survival Distribution Functions
question: (Mixed RV) The distribution function of a random variable $X$, is given by $F(x) = \begin{cases} 0, & x < 0 \\ \frac{x}{2}, & 0\le x < 1 \\ \frac{2}{3}, & 1\le x < 2 \\ \frac{11}{12}, & 2\le x < 3 \\ 1, & 3\le x \end{cases}$ (a) Graph $F(x)$. (b) Compute $\Pr(X < 3)$. (c) Compute $\Pr(X = 1)$. (d) Compute $\Pr(X > \frac{1}{2})$ (e) Compute $\Pr(2 < X\le 4)$.
explanation: (a) The graph is given in Figure 25.3. (b) $\Pr(X < 3) = \lim_{n\to\infty} \Pr\left(\left\{X\le 3-\frac{1}{n}\right\}\right) = \lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(3-\frac{1}{n}\right) = \frac{11}{12}$. (c) $\Pr(X = 1) = \Pr(X\le 1) - \Pr(X < 1) = F(1) - \lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(1-\frac{1}{n}\right) = \frac{2}{3} - \frac{1}{2} = \frac{1}{6}$. (d) $\Pr(X > \frac{1}{2}) = 1-\Pr(X\le \frac{1}{2}) = 1-F\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) = \frac{3}{4}$. (e) $\Pr(2 < X\le 4) = F(4)-F(2) = \frac{1}{12}$. Figure 25.3
